questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A particle is launched from a height $8 R$ above the surface of earth and it is given a speed $\sqrt{\frac{G M}{8 R}}$ parallel to the surface then
options: $\text { It will escape fro the gravitational field of earth. }$
$\text { it will follow a circular orbit. }$
$\text { it will follow an elliptical orbit. }$
$\text { the point of launching will be the perigee of its orbit }$
questions.solution:
Speed is more than the orbital but less than the escape velocity therefore an orbit will form. The speed is perpendicular to the radius.
It must be either an apogee or a perigee. But since the speed will be increasing, the launch point must be the perigee.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Two objects of mass m and 4 m are at rest at an infinite separation. They move towards each other under mutual attraction. If G is the universal gravitational constant. Then at separation r .
options: The sum of energy of the two objects is negative.
Their relative velocity of approach is $\left(\frac{10 G \mathrm{~m}}{r}\right)^{1 / 2}$ in magnitude
The sum of kinetic energy of the objects is $\frac{4 \mathrm{Gm}^2}{r}$.
The sum of angular momenta of both the objects is zero about any point.
questions.solution:
Initially potential and kinetic both energies zero and from conservation of mechanical energy total energy of the two object zero
Further, decrease in P.E. = increase in K.E.
$\begin{aligned}
& \frac{G(m)(4 m)}{r}=\frac{1}{2} \mu v_r^2 \\
& \quad v_r=\sqrt{\frac{10 G m}{r}} \\
& \text { Total K.E. }=\frac{G(m)(4 m)}{r}=\frac{4 G m^2}{r}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A particle is dropped from a height equal to the radius of the earth above the tunnel dug through the earth as shown in the figure. $R$ : Radius of earth. M : Mass of earth.
options: Particle will oscillate through the earth to a height $R$ on both sides
Particle will execute simple harmonic motion
Motion of the particle is periodic
Particle passes the centre of earth with a speed $=\sqrt{\frac{2 \mathrm{GM}}{\mathrm{R}}}$
questions.solution:
From $\operatorname{COE}(A)$ is correct.
The force outside the earth varies as inverse square of the distance.
$\therefore \quad$ motion is not simple harmonic. However, from symmetry of motion, the motion will be periodic.
$\therefore \quad(C)$ is correct.
From COE
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{1}{2} m v^2=-\frac{G M m}{2 R}-\left(-\frac{3 G M m}{2 R}\right) \\ \Rightarrow & v=\sqrt{\frac{2 G M}{R}}\end{aligned}$
$\therefore \quad(A),(C)$ and (D) are correct.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
